claims.interpretation: The evidence supports a conditional posture rather than a broad causal conclusion.
claims.statement: The Uptrend Under Pressure regime remains the primary operating frame. Index structure, participation, leadership led by Energy, and cross-asset proxy evidence should be used together; incomplete confirmation favors selective exposure and explicit invalidation levels.
claims.trader_implication: Favor setups that confirm on price, participation, and volume; reduce risk when those signals diverge.

claims.interpretation: A point-in-time score needs historical direction and cross-signal confirmation before it can support a stronger conclusion.
claims.statement: Current risk, sentiment, and participation are evaluated as separate observations; disagreement between them is treated as counter-evidence.
claims.trader_implication: Keep position size conditional on benchmark support and participation rather than relying on a single risk score.

limitations: Direct Treasury yield and yield-curve histories are unavailable; ETF bond prices are labelled as proxies.
High-yield and investment-grade spread histories are unavailable; HYG is labelled as a price proxy.
No external research URLs, validated causal-attribution model, or historical analogues are available.
Theme histories use the current reviewed basket unless a versioned historical membership record exists.
Unsourced economic and earnings event strings are excluded from V6.
VIX history and term structure are unavailable as durable sourced series.

monitoring_conditions.action_implication: Maintain selective exposure while support holds; reduce risk on a confirmed break.
monitoring_conditions.rationale: Tests whether the primary trend remains intact.

monitoring_conditions.action_implication: Become more aggressive only as participation broadens.
monitoring_conditions.rationale: Tests whether index strength is broadly supported.
monitoring_conditions.threshold_or_condition: Short- and medium-horizon participation improve together

monitoring_conditions.action_implication: Favor groups with both price and participation confirmation.
monitoring_conditions.rationale: Separates durable leadership from concentrated ETF movement.
monitoring_conditions.threshold_or_condition: Relative strength and constituent breadth agree

monitoring_conditions.action_implication: Reduce size when cross-asset disagreement grows.
monitoring_conditions.rationale: Provides cross-asset confirmation without asserting causality.
monitoring_conditions.threshold_or_condition: Credit and defensive proxies do not contradict equities

invalidation: Benchmark price breaks medium-term support while breadth deteriorates.
Credit and volatility proxies stop confirming the prevailing risk posture.
scenarios.label: Primary
scenarios.operating_response: Remain selective and add risk only to confirmed setups.
scenarios.position_sizing_implication: Use normal-to-reduced size according to setup quality and invalidation distance.
required_conditions: Benchmark structure remains above medium-term support.
risk_conditions: Volatility and credit proxies remain contained

benchmark_levels: Close above the recent swing high
breadth_conditions: Short- and medium-horizon breadth rise together
invalidation: Breakout fails back into the prior range.
scenarios.label: Bullish Alternative
scenarios.operating_response: Increase exposure gradually through confirmed entries rather than chasing extension.
scenarios.position_sizing_implication: Scale only after confirmation and retain explicit stops.

invalidation: Benchmarks reclaim support with improving breadth.
scenarios.label: Bearish Alternative
scenarios.operating_response: Reduce gross exposure, avoid weak breakouts, and preserve liquidity.
scenarios.position_sizing_implication: Use reduced size or remain uncommitted until evidence stabilizes.
required_conditions: Participation and risk evidence deteriorate together.
risk_conditions: Risk history worsens or defensive proxies strengthen

paragraphs: The Uptrend Under Pressure regime remains the primary operating frame. Index structure, participation, leadership led by Energy, and cross-asset proxy evidence should be used together; incomplete confirmation favors selective exposure and explicit invalidation levels.
sections.purpose: State the posture and define what confirms or invalidates it.
sections.title: Cover and Current Thesis

paragraphs: Baseline established. The primary thesis is conditional: market structure establishes the trend, breadth tests participation, leadership tests concentration, and supported cross-asset proxies provide confirmation or counter-evidence.

The operating posture is selective. Setups deserve capital when their own price and volume evidence agrees with the broader market; disagreement is a reason to reduce size, wait for confirmation, or preserve liquidity.

The next report should become more constructive only if benchmark structure and participation improve together. A break of medium-term support accompanied by weaker breadth and defensive cross-asset behavior would invalidate the current view.
sections.purpose: Connect structure, participation, leadership, cross-asset evidence and risk into one argument.
sections.title: Executive Summary

paragraphs: Large index charts carry the structure argument. Moving averages are descriptive reference levels, while volume and relative ratios test whether apparent index strength is extending across styles.
sections.purpose: Test trend quality across capitalization and style benchmarks.
sections.title: Major Index Structure

paragraphs: Participation is evaluated across short, medium, and long horizons rather than collapsed into one status. Net advances and highs-versus-lows add daily internal evidence, while missing histories are disclosed rather than inferred.
sections.purpose: Determine whether constituent participation confirms benchmark price.
sections.title: Breadth and Participation

paragraphs: Multi-horizon returns identify persistence, rotation tails show direction, and constituent breadth tests whether ETF movement is representative. A quadrant label alone is not treated as sufficient evidence.
sections.purpose: Separate established leadership from emerging, weakening, and concentrated moves.
sections.title: Leadership and Rotation

paragraphs: Cross-asset analysis uses adjusted ETF price proxies on aligned dates. Treasury and high-yield instruments are explicitly not presented as direct yields or credit spreads, and relationships are observational rather than causal.
sections.purpose: Test whether supported market proxies agree with the equity posture.
sections.title: Cross-Asset and Macro Confirmation

paragraphs: Historical context is shown only from compatible immutable reports. Unsupported volatility term structure and spread histories are omitted, so the section distinguishes current observations from unavailable long-run evidence.

The principal risk is a joint deterioration in benchmark structure and participation. Current sentiment or risk scores are supporting observations only; they do not replace the price, breadth, and cross-asset confirmation framework.
sections.purpose: Describe current risk and its direction using available history.
sections.title: Risk, Volatility, Credit and Sentiment

paragraphs: Scenarios use qualitative likelihood labels because no validated probability model is asserted by V6. Each path identifies confirmation, invalidation, leadership and operating response from evidence already introduced.
sections.purpose: Translate prior evidence into conditional market paths.
sections.title: Scenario Framework

paragraphs: The candidate matrix ranks the frozen watchlist by setup state and freshness. Deep dives use supported price, volume and level evidence; stale or partial records remain monitoring-only.
sections.purpose: Prioritize personalized monitoring without promoting stale or partial evidence.
sections.title: Watchlist and Security Research

paragraphs: The checklist links every condition to a metric, rationale and action implication. It avoids generic instructions and does not substitute for personalized investment advice.
sections.purpose: Convert the thesis into explicit monitoring and risk conditions.
sections.title: Next-Session Operating Plan

paragraphs: All interpretations are system-generated from the frozen evidence registry. Figures cite provider or internal snapshot sources, carry timestamps and quality states, and omit unsupported series cleanly.
sections.purpose: Make provenance, transformations and unavailable evidence auditable.
sections.quality_note: Direct Treasury yield and yield-curve histories are unavailable; ETF bond prices are labelled as proxies.; High-yield and investment-grade spread histories are unavailable; HYG is labelled as a price proxy.; No external research URLs, validated causal-attribution model, or historical analogues are available.; Theme histories use the current reviewed basket unless a versioned historical membership record exists.; Unsourced economic and earnings event strings are excluded from V6.; VIX history and term structure are unavailable as durable sourced series.
sections.title: Methodology, Sources and Limitations

thesis.concise_thesis: The Uptrend Under Pressure regime remains the primary operating frame. Index structure, participation, leadership led by Energy, and cross-asset proxy evidence should be used together; incomplete confirmation favors selective exposure and explicit invalidation levels.
confirmation_conditions: Benchmark price holds above medium-term support while participation improves.
Leadership broadens beyond the current leading groups with confirming volume.
invalidation_conditions: Benchmark price breaks medium-term support while breadth deteriorates.
Credit and volatility proxies stop confirming the prevailing risk posture.

title: Daily Market Intelligence Briefing
